# Implementation Plan: Bind remove picks to disambiguation session

**Branch**: `008-bind-remove-pick-session` | **Date**: 2026-04-26 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)  
**Input**: Feature specification from `specs/008-bind-remove-pick-session/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` workflow. Revalidated after `/speckit.clarify` (see [spec.md](./spec.md) **Clarifications**). *Re-runs of `setup-plan.sh` were not used here: that script overwrites this file with the empty template.*

## Summary

Remove-album disambiguation must resolve each pick (especially inline `rmp:` callbacks) against the **specific** disambiguation session the user is acting on, not the listener’s *latest* open row in `disambiguation_sessions`. Today the callback embeds `rmp:<session_id>:<index>` but the session id is ignored, so a stale message’s button can remove the wrong row after a newer `/remove` or other flow supersedes the old session.

**Approach:** Load disambiguation by **primary key** (and **listener ownership** + **non-expired**) for **all** **inline** (`rmp:`) picks. Return safe, non-destructive user copy when the session is missing or not a `remove_saved` payload (**FR-003** in [spec.md](./spec.md)). For **typed** `1`–`99` replies (no session id in text), the product uses the **latest** open session **only** when it unmarshals to `kind: remove_saved`, and must not mis-apply a remove index to album disambiguation (**FR-005**, **US3** in [spec.md](./spec.md)). v1 does **not** require inferring which of two *historical* on-screen remove lists a bare number referred to; **inline** actions are the supported path for that guarantee (see [research.md](./research.md) §3).

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Go 1.25.x (module `github.com/mellomaths/lifesoundtrack/bot`)  
**Primary Dependencies**: `github.com/jackc/pgx/v5`, `github.com/go-telegram/bot`, existing bot `internal/core` + `internal/store`  
**Storage**: PostgreSQL — `disambiguation_sessions` (id, listener_id, candidates JSON, expires_at), `listeners`, `saved_albums`  
**Testing**: `go test ./...` under `bot/`; table-driven unit tests in `core` and `store`; adapter tests in [tasks.md](./tasks.md) (T014) for `rmp:` callback wiring, plus existing parser coverage in `routing_test.go` where applicable  
**Target Platform**: Linux (deployed bot); local dev on Windows/macOS/Linux  
**Project Type**: Telegram bot — domain logic in `bot/internal/core`, persistence in `bot/internal/store`, Telegram in `bot/internal/adapter/telegram`  
**Performance Goals**: N/A — a few extra indexed lookups by session UUID per pick; no hot-path streaming  
**Constraints**: Telegram `callback_data` ≤ 64 bytes (existing `rmp:` + UUID + index design already fits); no cross-listener data access  
**Scale/Scope**: Single feature; primary files: `remove_saved.go` (core), `store/disambig*.go` (+ new query and tests), `adapter/telegram/run.go`. `SaveService` / `SavePersistence` in [save_album.go](../../bot/internal/core/save_album.go) change **only** if test doubles (`memStore`) on `*store.Store` need new methods for `LibraryService` tests — not the default path for this feature.

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Passes before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design: **PASS** (re-checked 2026-04-26 after spec clarifications: testing split inline vs typed; no new REST or container surfaces).*

- **Code quality**: Scoped change; follow existing `gofmt` / project lint; no new global suppressions.
- **REST API**: Not in scope (Telegram + internal core only); N/A.
- **Testing**: **Inline** path: regression for two `remove_saved` sessions + stale callback (**SC-001** session-bound scope in [spec.md](./spec.md)). **Typed** path: `kind` safety and single open `remove_saved` (**US3**); see [tasks.md](./tasks.md) T006, T010, **T014**. `go test` in CI.
- **User experience**: Stale/invalid pick messages align with [spec.md](./spec.md) **FR-003** and NFR (clear, non-destructive); **FR-005** / **Clarifications** set expectations for text vs inline.
- **Monitoring**: N/A for mandatory new metrics; optional warn logs on stale callback (per spec MAY).
- **Logging**: No secrets; optional structured context (session id, reason) for rejected picks.
- **Performance**: N/A with justification (see Technical Context).
- **Containerization**: No change to `Dockerfile` / compose unless a runnable entrypoint changes (not expected).
- **Documentation accuracy**: This plan and [research.md](./research.md) reference real paths under `bot/` and `specs/008-…`.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/008-bind-remove-pick-session/
├── plan.md              # This file
├── research.md          # Phase 0
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1
├── contracts/           # Phase 1
│   └── remove-pick-binding.md
└── tasks.md             # /speckit.tasks — implementation checklist (present)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
bot/
├── cmd/bot/
├── internal/
│   ├── adapter/telegram/     # run.go: pass session id from rmp: into core
│   ├── core/                 # TryProcessRemovePick + optional session id; remove_saved
│   └── store/                # New: load disambiguation session by id + listener + open
│       └── (tests)
```

**Structure Decision**: All behavior stays in the existing Go module. No new top-level services; extend `*store.Store` and `LibraryService` only as needed for session-keyed resolution.
